channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Elon Musk’s Grok-2 is now free—and it’s a mess
item.description: Late Friday night, Elon Musk’s xAI opened up Grok-2 access to the masses. The model isn’t all that different from the original Grok, though the company claims it runs faster and has improved accuracy. And yet, X users have spent the weekend dunking on it.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia's Advanced DLSS Technology and Neural Rendering Capabilities Set for CES 2025
item.description: Nvidia's upcoming revelations at CES 2025 include advanced DLSS technology, possibly DLSS 4, and new neural rendering capabilities. The tech is set to be showcased alongside RTX 5000 GPUs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The OnePlus 13 and 13R could be the first real Samsung Galaxy S25 competitors
item.description: OnePlus has announced a January 7 release date for its latest flagship, the OnePlus 13, as well as its midrange companion, the OnePlus 13R.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Municipal Transportation Company in Madrid Leverages Technology for More Connected, Sustainable, and User-Centric Mobility
item.description: Madrid's Municipal Transportation Company (EMT) is leveraging technology to improve the user experience and efficiency of its services, using AI-powered solutions to manage customer inquiries and optimize bus operations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: El Ministerio de la Presidencia, Justicia y Relaciones con las Cortes gana el ‘CIO 100 Awards’ al mejor Proyecto de Inteligencia Artificial
item.description: El premio al mejor proyecto de gestión de datos e inteligencia artificial (IA) en 2024 de los CIO 100 Awards ha recalado en el Ministerio de la Presidencia, Justicia y Relaciones con las Cortes por un despliegue de servicios que ha permitido optimizar la gestión de grandes cantidades de información
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: IT/OT convergence propels zero-trust security efforts
item.description: Zero-trust security is essential to enterprises that are converging operational technology (OT) with IT infrastructure. New research from Enterprise Management Associates (EMA) identified how this convergence influences zero-trust strategy and implementation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia App Slowdowns Hit Some Gamers - A Performance Issue to Watch Out For
item.description: Nvidia App slowdowns have been reported by some gamers, causing performance issues with games like Black Myth: Wukong and The Talos Principle 2.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Why AI company Databricks just scored one of the biggest funding rounds in history
item.description: The San Francisco-based data analytics company Databricks has raised a stunning $10 billion funding round, which values the company at $62 billion and likely puts it on a path toward an IPO.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Bitdefender Unveils Scamio, AI-Powered Chatbot to Combat Discord Scams - Cybersecurity News
item.description: Bitdefender launches Scamio, a free AI-powered chatbot designed to protect Discord users from scams in real-time. The platform analyzes content for malicious intent and offers personalized recommendations to enhance user safety.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools
item.description: EPA's CIO says modernized tools and integrated teams help the agency meet the changing needs of the public more easily and consistently. The post EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: How Anyscale cofounder Robert Nishihara is smoothing AI’s complexities
item.description: As businesses reinvent workflows and products with AI, many such initiatives have stalled or failed to reach production. It can be really hard to implement the technology.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Europe takes on Starlink with $11.5 billion IRIS² satellite constellation
item.description: The European Union is planning its own satellite constellation, IRIS², as part of a €10.6 billion ($11.1 billion ) initiative to provide secure, resilient connectivity to businesses, governments, and citizens.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The End of an Era: Equinix to Discontinue Bare-Metal IaaS Offering
item.description: Equinix, a leading provider of hosting and colocation services, announces the discontinuation of its bare-metal infrastructure-as-a-service (IaaS) offering by June 2026.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Gemini Code Assist tools target developer productivity from within IDEs
item.description: Google has launched new Gemini Code Assist tools that are designed to help developers improve their productivity from within an integrated development environment (IDE), such as Visual Studio Code or JetBrains.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Operational silos could overwhelm more enterprises in the future
item.description: Operational silos across NetOps, SecOps and CloudOps teams could lead to critical security vulnerabilities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Uh oh... Zotac just leaked Nvidia’s next-gen launch line-up, including RTX 5090 GPU with 32GB of VRAM
item.description: Zotac accidentally spills the details on Nvidia’s initial RTX 5000 range – but there’s no RTX 5060 to be seen.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: John Kirby drone statement 'very misleading at best': New Jersey rep
item.description: Rep. Chris Smith (R- N.J.) slammed White House national security spokesperson John Kirby’s statement about the recent mysterious drone sightings on the East Coast. “Well, I think it's very misleading at best," Smith said in an interview on NewsNation's "The Hill" on Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Top 5 use cases for small language models
item.description: Since ChatGPT arrived in late 2022, large language models (LLMs) have continued to raise the bar for what generative AI systems can accomplish.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to contact the Rhode Island cyberattack call center after data breach
item.description: Rhode Island officials urged residents to take immediate steps to protect themselves on Saturday, warning that the hackers could release personal data.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: The case for private clouds for data management in government
item.description: When it comes to taking advantage of AI, storing data in a cost-effective, massively scalable private or hybrid cloud solution is often the best choice. The post The case for private clouds for data management in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CompTIA introduces foundational tech certification
item.description: CompTIA has created a certification designed to provide individuals with broad foundational and digital skills that can be used to build a career in technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Weaponizing generative AI
item.description: Well, that didn’t last long. Generative AI has existed just a few short years, but already we seem to be alternating between bouts of disillusionment and euphoria.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI-powered deception: The sneaky macOS malware masquerading as your next video call
item.description: Artificial intelligence is making life easier for cybercriminals, allowing them to create elaborate scams to trick people. Kurt the Cyberguy explains how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Visual Studio Code adds overtype mode, paste with imports
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.96, the latest version of Microsoft’s popular code editor, has arrived with an overtype mode and a “paste with imports” feature for JavaScript and TypeScript.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, Fox 5
